title: Annual Update - HPEK (1)
text:
College of the Siskiyous Annual Program Update
HEALTH, PHYSICAL EDUCATION, and KINESIOLOGY
1. Describe annual progress / changes / events in the program.
a. Submitted the Physical Education AA program sequence into eLuman
i. Created a sequence for students
ii. Created PLO’s
iii. Created more options for students
b. Created new transfer level lecture courses for the program
i. KINE 1003 – CPR, First Aid, and Emergencies
ii. KINE 1004 – Psychology of Sport
iii. KINE 1005 – Sport and United State Society
iv. KINE 1006 – Sports Management
v. HEA 1009 – Human Sexuality
c. Added DE addendum to all of our Off-Season PEMA courses
d. Updated / Changed the prefix on a few courses
i. PEMA 1031 to KINE 1031 Sports Officiating
ii. PEMA 1038 to KINE 1003 – CPR, First Aid, and Emergencies
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
a. SLO assessment is done and kept by instructors. We need a place that instructors can
put their data they collect on SLO assessment.
b. PLO’s – We just created new PLO’s with our program that we put into eLuman.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
a. SLO assessment input – Where do they go? How can we make it as easy as possible for
the adjunct instructor teaching one course to input?
b. ADT – We had to develop a CPR course so we can get aligned with an ADT in our area.
We are not sure if an ADT will help our area or not. Several of the course are not taught
in our area. The feeling is we would be better with a local degree. Most of our students
transfer to colleges out of state. To meet the NCAA requirements, most of the time the
student just needs the local AA degree.
c. Adjunct instructors – We have several adjunct instructors that can teach courses in our
department. All are head coaches. If we had the ability to provide a stipend for head
coaching duties that would free up load for them to be able to teach. Right now, all of
our adjunct head coaches do not have enough room in their load to teach courses
outside of their sport. This is detrimental to the growth of our program.
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications.
a. We need full-time faculty in our area. In order to grow our Kinesiology degree, we need
to be able to offer the courses to complete the degree on a regular basis. We have only
replaced one of the last four resignations/retirements of full-time faculty in our area.
All of our adjunct instructors do the best they can in recruiting. Full-time faculty have

-- 1 of 2 --

more time and resources available to recruit and more load available to teach. If we
want more enrollment we should have more full-time faculty in our area. Finding ways
to be creative with how we pay for coaching duties would be helpful (Stipends, Release
Time, Summer adjunct)
(Submit a CQIP if you are requesting a budget allocation that falls outside of regular yearly budget
allocations).

-- 2 of 2 --
